questions.question: What is the Department of Defense's definition of cyberspace?
options: A global domain within the information environment consisting of the interdependent networks of information technology infrastructures and resident data
The collection of all military computer networks and systems used for command and control
Any electronic medium used to transmit classified information between government agencies
The physical infrastructure of servers, routers, and cables that support internet communications

questions.question: Which organization serves as the Air Force's primary component for cyberspace operations?
options: Air Combat Command (ACC)
Air Force Space Command (AFSPC)
16th Air Force (Air Forces Cyber)
25th Air Force

questions.question: What are the three types of cyberspace operations recognized by the Department of Defense?
options: Network attack, network defense, and network exploitation
Offensive cyberspace operations, defensive cyberspace operations, and DODIN operations
Cyber warfare, cyber intelligence, and cyber security
Strategic cyber operations, tactical cyber operations, and operational cyber operations

questions.question: What does DODIN stand for?
options: Department of Defense Intelligence Network
Distributed Operations Data Integration Network
Department of Defense Information Network
Defense Operational Data Infrastructure Network

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Offensive Cyberspace Operations (OCO)?
options: To monitor friendly networks for unauthorized access
To maintain and configure Department of Defense networks
To train cyber operators in simulated environments
To project power by the application of force in or through cyberspace

questions.question: Defensive Cyberspace Operations (DCO) are conducted to:
options: Defend the Department of Defense or other friendly cyberspace and protect data, networks, and systems
Launch preemptive strikes against known adversary cyber infrastructure
Develop new encryption algorithms for military communications
Recruit and train civilian cybersecurity professionals for government service

questions.question: Which of the following best describes DODIN operations?
options: Offensive actions taken against adversary networks to degrade their capabilities
Operations to secure, configure, operate, extend, maintain, and sustain DOD cyberspace
Intelligence gathering operations conducted through cyber means
Joint operations combining cyber and kinetic effects against enemy targets

questions.question: What is Operations Security (OPSEC)?
options: The encryption of all data stored on government systems
Physical security measures for military installations
A classified program for monitoring foreign communications
A process of identifying critical information and analyzing friendly actions to deny adversaries knowledge of operations

questions.question: What is the first step in the OPSEC process?
options: Analyze threats
Identify critical information
Assess risk
Analyze vulnerabilities

questions.question: Communications Security (COMSEC) is designed to protect which of the following?
options: Only voice communications over telephone lines
Physical documents marked as classified
Telecommunications and information systems from unauthorized access
Only email communications on government networks

questions.question: Which type of cyber threat actor is typically the most well-resourced and persistent?
options: Hacktivists
Nation-state actors
Script kiddies
Insider threats

questions.question: What is phishing?
options: A method of encrypting data to prevent unauthorized access
A hardware attack that intercepts network traffic at the router level
A technique used to probe network defenses for open ports
A social engineering attack that uses deceptive emails or messages to trick users into revealing sensitive information

questions.question: Spear phishing differs from general phishing in that it:
options: Targets specific individuals or organizations with personalized messages
Uses phone calls instead of emails
Only targets government networks
Involves physical access to the target's computer

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Risk Management Framework (RMF) in cybersecurity?
options: To replace all firewalls with advanced intrusion detection systems
To provide a structured process for integrating security and risk management into information systems
To eliminate all cyber threats from government networks
To train all military personnel as cybersecurity specialists

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of good cyber hygiene?
options: Sharing passwords with trusted coworkers for convenience
Using the same password across all systems for ease of memory
Connecting personal devices to government networks to increase productivity
Using strong, unique passwords and enabling multi-factor authentication

questions.question: What is the primary role of U.S. Cyber Command (USCYBERCOM)?
options: To direct, synchronize, and coordinate cyberspace planning and operations to defend and advance national interests
To develop commercial cybersecurity software for government agencies
To conduct only defensive operations on behalf of the intelligence community
To manage the procurement of information technology hardware for the DOD

questions.question: What is an Advanced Persistent Threat (APT)?
options: A one-time malware infection that is quickly detected and removed
A type of firewall used to protect critical infrastructure
A prolonged and targeted cyberattack in which an adversary gains and maintains unauthorized access to a network
An automated software update that patches system vulnerabilities

questions.question: Which layer of cyberspace includes the physical network components such as cables, routers, and servers?
options: Logical layer
Physical network layer
Cyber-persona layer
Application layer

questions.question: The cyber-persona layer of cyberspace consists of:
options: Physical hardware devices connected to the network
Routing protocols and IP addressing schemes
The identities and accounts that represent people and entities in cyberspace
Encryption algorithms used to protect data in transit

questions.question: What does the logical network layer of cyberspace encompass?
options: The elements of the network that are related to one another in ways that are abstracted from the physical network
Only the operating systems installed on network devices
The people who interact with cyberspace systems
Exclusively the wireless transmission frequencies used by military assets

questions.question: What is the purpose of an Intrusion Detection System (IDS)?
options: To encrypt all outbound network traffic
To physically prevent unauthorized users from accessing terminals
To monitor network traffic for suspicious activity and alert administrators
To automatically launch counterattacks against intruders

questions.question: Which of the following best describes social engineering in the context of cybersecurity?
options: Manipulating people into divulging confidential information or performing actions that compromise security
Designing user-friendly interfaces for government computer systems
Developing social media strategies for public affairs offices
Programming automated bots to interact with users on social media platforms

questions.question: What is the correct order of the OPSEC five-step process?
options: Analyze threats, identify critical information, analyze vulnerabilities, assess risk, apply countermeasures
Assess risk, analyze threats, apply countermeasures, analyze vulnerabilities, identify critical information
Apply countermeasures, identify critical information, analyze threats, assess risk, analyze vulnerabilities
Identify critical information, analyze threats, analyze vulnerabilities, assess risk, apply countermeasures

questions.question: Critical Infrastructure Protection (CIP) in the cyber domain focuses on:
options: Building physical barriers around data centers
Safeguarding systems and assets whose disruption would have a debilitating impact on security, the economy, or public health and safety
Classifying all government information at the top secret level
Replacing all legacy systems with new hardware every two years

questions.question: What is the primary goal of cyber incident response?
options: To permanently shut down compromised networks
To publicly disclose all security breaches within 24 hours
To replace all hardware on the affected network
To quickly identify, contain, eradicate, and recover from cybersecurity incidents

questions.question: Authorized use of government information systems means that users must:
options: Use government systems only for official or authorized purposes and comply with all applicable policies
Have unlimited personal use of government systems during non-duty hours
Share login credentials with supervisors for transparency
Install any software that improves personal productivity on government machines

questions.question: What is malware?
options: Hardware components that have been physically damaged
Authorized security testing tools used by network administrators
Software intentionally designed to cause damage to a computer, server, or network
A type of network configuration used to isolate sensitive data

questions.question: Which of the following is a component of COMSEC?
options: Operational planning for kinetic strikes
Cryptographic security, including the use of encryption to protect information
Physical fitness standards for communications personnel
Procurement of commercial cell phones for government use

questions.question: Transmission security, a component of COMSEC, protects communications from:
options: Physical theft of communication devices
Budget shortfalls in communications programs
Interception and exploitation through measures other than cryptography
Software licensing violations

questions.question: Why is cyberspace considered a warfighting domain?
options: Because it is used exclusively for intelligence collection
Because all military operations have some dependency on cyberspace, and adversaries can contest it
Because it replaces the need for air, land, and sea operations
Because only military personnel have access to cyberspace

questions.question: What is a denial-of-service (DoS) attack?
options: An attempt to make a machine or network resource unavailable to its intended users by overwhelming it with traffic
Denying physical access to a server room
A policy that restricts users from accessing certain websites
A method for employees to request service outages for maintenance

questions.question: What distinguishes Defensive Cyberspace Operations-Response Actions (DCO-RA) from other defensive measures?
options: DCO-RA are purely administrative actions such as updating security policies
DCO-RA are limited to monitoring network traffic for anomalies
DCO-RA involve deliberate actions taken outside the defended network to defeat specific threats
DCO-RA focus exclusively on training personnel in cyber awareness

questions.question: Multi-factor authentication enhances security by:
options: Allowing users to bypass password requirements
Requiring users to verify their identity using two or more independent factors
Automatically encrypting all files on a user's computer
Replacing passwords with biometric scans exclusively

questions.question: What is the function of a firewall in network defense?
options: To provide wireless connectivity to all network users
To store backup copies of critical data
To manage user account creation and deletion
To monitor and control incoming and outgoing network traffic based on predetermined security rules

questions.question: Insider threats in cybersecurity refer to:
options: Threats posed by individuals within the organization who have authorized access and may intentionally or unintentionally cause harm
External hackers who have breached the network perimeter
Automated malware that spreads without human interaction
Foreign intelligence agencies conducting espionage operations

questions.question: What is the significance of the Common Access Card (CAC) in DOD cybersecurity?
options: It serves only as a physical identification badge
It provides encrypted storage for personal files
It serves as the standard identification and authentication token for accessing DOD networks and systems
It automatically updates software on government computers

questions.question: Cyber warfare integration with traditional military operations is important because:
options: Cyber operations can only be conducted independently of other military actions
Cyber effects can enhance or be synchronized with kinetic operations across all domains
Traditional military operations are no longer relevant in modern warfare
Cyber capabilities eliminate the need for intelligence gathering

questions.question: When a user suspects a cybersecurity incident on a government system, the correct first action is to:
options: Attempt to resolve the issue personally before reporting it
Immediately disconnect the system from the internet and wipe the hard drive
Ignore the issue unless it directly affects mission operations
Report the incident to the appropriate cybersecurity authority and follow established incident response procedures

questions.question: What is the principle of least privilege in cybersecurity?
options: Granting users only the minimum access necessary to perform their duties
Providing all users with administrator-level access for maximum efficiency
Restricting network access to only senior leadership
Allowing unrestricted access during duty hours and restricted access after hours

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the concept of defense in depth in cybersecurity?
options: Relying on a single, highly advanced security tool to protect the entire network
Focusing all security resources on protecting the network perimeter
Employing multiple layers of security controls throughout an information system to provide redundancy
Delegating all cybersecurity responsibilities to a single office or individual
